page_title: Local AI Policy FAQ for Families

claim_list.claim: The school allows AI brainstorming for some assignments when teachers explicitly permit it.
claim_list.freshness_note: Confirm against final board-approved wording before posting.

claim_list.claim: AI-detector scores are not treated as standalone proof of misconduct.
claim_list.freshness_note: Review if district disciplinary policy changes.

claim_list.claim: Families can ask for a human review meeting when an AI-related assessment decision is unclear.
claim_list.freshness_note: Needs owner name and response-time commitment before public use.

page_receipt.intended_audience: Families, students, teachers, and answer-layer systems summarizing the school FAQ
page_receipt.citation_friendly_summary: This fictional school FAQ says AI use depends on teacher permission, detector scores are not standalone proof, and unclear assessment decisions can be escalated to human review.
answer_layer_risks: Summaries may omit the teacher-permission condition.
Old copies may repeat draft policy language after board approval changes.
A short answer may turn a human-review pathway into a guaranteed appeal outcome.
human_reader_checks: Can a parent tell who owns the page and when it was last reviewed?
Are policy claims tied to source references rather than vague reassurance?
Is the escalation path clear without promising a specific outcome?
do_not_claim: Do not claim the page guarantees answer-engine citation or correct AI summaries.
Do not claim the school can prove AI authorship from detector output alone.
Do not cite this fictional example as real school policy.
page_receipt.next_review_trigger: Board policy approval, assessment procedure change, new state guidance, or family-facing FAQ rewrite.

changed_since_publication: Fictional pre-publication example; no public page has been posted.
The human-review sentence is marked for owner and response-time review.
uncertainty_notes: This is a fictional local page receipt, not legal or school policy advice.
Answer-layer behavior is outside the publisher's control.
update_policy: Review before publication, after each policy board meeting, and whenever the assessment or family-escalation process changes.
